checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=1430100000 (2017-05-28) vs 1360800000 (2016-05-29), +5.1%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet fell from 16563100000 (FY2016) to 15619800000 (FY2017), -5.7%
ReceivablesNetCurrent=1684200000 (2018-05-27) vs 1430100000 (2017-05-28), +17.8%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only from 15619800000 (FY2017) to 15740400000 (FY2018), +0.8%
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2018-12-19, balance sheet as of 2018-11-25 with prior FY comparisons; revenue from 10-K 0001193125-18-209377

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=7445100000 (2018-05-27) vs 4530400000 (2017-05-28), +64.4%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only +0.8% over the same period (15740400000 FY2018 vs 15619800000 FY2017)
Assets=30624000000 (2018-05-27) vs Assets=21812600000 (2017-05-28), a jump consistent with a major acquisition (Blue Buffalo) rather than organic growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K cash flow statement / income statement FY2018

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=1642200000 (2018-05-27) vs 1483600000 (2017-05-28), +10.7%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only +0.8% over FY2018 vs FY2017

Gross margin (GrossProfit/Revenue) computed from CostOfGoodsSold=10312900000 and SalesRevenueGoodsNet=15740400000 (FY2018) = 34.5%, versus 35.6% in FY2017 (CostOfGoodsSold=10056000000, Revenue=15619800000) - modest compression consistent with acquisition mix shift, not a major distortion

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss fell from 1824400000 (FY2014) to 1221300000 (FY2015), a 33% decline, then rebounded to 1697400000 (FY2016), +39%, showing meaningful earnings volatility rather than artificial smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=24300000 (2017-05-28) declined from 29600000 (2016-05-29) even as ReceivablesNetCurrent rose from 1360800000 (2016-05-29) to 1430100000 (2017-05-28)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: No NT 10-K/NT 10-Q filings appear in the chronology near the 2019-02-20 cutoff; 8-K/A filings on 2018-06-27, 2018-06-29, and 2019-02-08 coincide with the Blue Buffalo acquisition period and appear to reflect purchase-accounting related amendments rather than restatements

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series data spans annual and quarterly points from fiscal 2008 (CostOfGoodsSold start 2007-05-28) through fiscal Q2 2019 (period end 2018-11-25), covering revenue, receivables, inventory, goodwill, intangibles, cash flow, and equity across ~10 fiscal years

top_signals: Receivables grew much faster than revenue in FY2017 (+5.1% vs -5.7% revenue) and FY2018 (+17.8% vs +0.8% revenue), though largely explained by the Blue Buffalo acquisition rather than channel-stuffing

Goodwill (+60.8%) and other intangibles (+64.4%) ballooned in FY2018 far outpacing 0.8% revenue growth, consistent with purchase accounting for the Blue Buffalo acquisition (Assets jumped from $21.8B to $30.6B) rather than an organic capitalization issue
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined in absolute dollars in FY2017 even as receivables grew, a mild reserve-adequacy flag but of low magnitude
Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income across all years examined (FY2014-FY2018), which argues against earnings overstatement via accrual manipulation

No late filings, NT notices, or financial restatements (10-K/A, 10-Q/A) appear in the chronology during the periods covered by the financial data; 8-K/A filings in 2018 appear tied to acquisition-related disclosures
